verdict_reasoning: HSPA1A (HSP70) is canonically a cytosolic molecular chaperone with no TM domain, GPI anchor, or secretory signal. However, extensive experimental evidence (cell-surface biotinylation, flow cytometry with cmHsp70.1 mAb on live non-permeabilized cells, confocal imaging) demonstrates that HSPA1A translocates to and embeds in the outer leaflet of the plasma membrane specifically in tumor cells and stress-exposed (heat shock, hypoxia) cells — but not on normal/unstressed cells. The surface pool is extracellularly accessible: a C-terminal epitope is exposed to the extracellular milieu, NK cells recognize it, and multiple theranostic programs (mAb cmHsp70.1, peptides, nanoparticles) directly target membrane-HSPA1A on viable tumor cells. The translocation mechanism is lipid-driven (PS, PI(4)P, PI(3)P, Gb3 in lipid rafts). Documented across glioblastoma, HNSCC, NSCLC, colorectal, pancreatic, osteosarcoma, AML, and ovarian cancer. Verdict: contextual / cell_state_induced (oncogenic transformation + cellular stress).
